verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted as a latent complex (LAP + mature TGFB1) but is covalently/non-covalently tethered to the cell surface via LTBPs and, critically, via GARP (LRRC32) and LRRC33 on regulatory T cells, platelets, and myeloid cells. LAP-TGFB1 is held to GARP by disulfide bonds, producing a wash-resistant, cell-surface-displayed latent TGFB1 complex. This is a canonical stable_surface_attachment scenario, and clinical antibodies (e.g., anti-GARP/TGFB1, anti-LAP) target this surface-tethered pool. Mature TGFB1 itself has no TM/GPI anchor, so verdict is contextual rather than yes.
